name: Permitted Use
text: The Tenant shall use the Premises solely for {{permitted_use}} and for no other purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Landlord. The Tenant shall: (a) not carry out any illegal or immoral activity on the Premises; (b) not store any hazardous, inflammable, or dangerous goods on the Premises without prior written consent; (c) not use the Premises in any manner that would constitute a nuisance to neighbours or violate applicable municipal regulations; (d) not display any sign, notice, or advertisement on the exterior of the Premises without the Landlord's prior written consent; (e) comply with all applicable laws, municipal regulations, and building bye-laws in respect of the use of the Premises; and (f) not sublet or licence the Premises or any part thereof without the prior written consent of the Landlord. Any breach of the permitted use clause shall entitle the Landlord to terminate this Agreement with immediate effect after written notice.
invalid_if: Permitted use restricted to single purpose without flexibility causing hardship
Use restriction so broad as to prevent all reasonable commercial activity
title: Permitted Use

jurisdiction_constraints.general: Commercial use in residentially-zoned area — municipal law restrictions apply

dispute_hotspots: Change of use — tenant converts residential to commercial without consent
Nuisance — tenant's business activity disturbs neighbours
Hazardous activity — lease void if use is unlawful under municipal laws
